originalTitle: Wildfire raging south of Paris could have been set deliberately, says minister
summary: The interior minister has said the fire near Paris could have been set deliberately and two people have been arrested.
content:
Firefighters battle wildfires in forest near Paris

The battle by firefighters in France to bring under control a large wildfire raging in forests south of the country's capital is entering its second day.

The Fontainebleau forest blaze, described by officials as "virulent" and of "exceptional scale", partially closed the country's main north-south highway.

Interior minister Laurent Nunez has indicated the blaze may have been deliberately set, after officials said the fire had raced across 800 hectares in the forest about 40 miles (60km) south-east of Paris.

On Monday, Nunez said that two people had been arrested in connection with the Fontainbleau blaze, and 59 across the country in connection with fires.

The Paris region is suffering through its third heatwave this year during a summer in which temperature records have been broken in several countries across Europe.

It is the first time firefighting planes had been sent up from the normally drier and hotter south of the country to tackle fires in the Paris region, Eric Brocardi, of France's national federation of firefighters, said.

Two firefighting helicopters and an observation aircraft had also been deployed, he added, according to French news agency Agence France-Presse.

"The aim is to save lives and property," he was quoted as saying.

Watch: Horses evacuated as fire approaches riding centre near Paris

Nunez suggested on Monday that authorities are exploring the possibility that the fire was started intentionally.

"There were about 10 fire ignition points within a perimeter of 1,000 meters, which suggests that it could have been deliberately set," he said in a statement reported by the AFP news agency.

Nunez told France 2's 8 PM News that two people were arrested for "deliberate or accidental arson" in the region.

Meanwhile, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote that the Fontainebleau forest had been "struck by a fire of exceptional magnitude".

"All available resources are being mobilised," he wrote on social media, as he expressed "solidarity" with local residents and praised firefighters tackling the blaze.

Earlier, a fire had also blocked a highway running east from Paris and disrupted a high-speed train line to the south of France.

Travellers were facing delays of up to six hours for trains arriving at or leaving from Paris's Gare de Lyon, French rail company SNCF said on Sunday evening.

Julien Marion, director general of civil security in France said on Friday that since the start of this year, wildfires have covered some 25,000 hectares of land in France.

The latest French heatwave has forced the temporary shutdown of three nuclear power stations to avoid the discharge of warm cooling water into overheated waterways.

Organisers of the Tour de France cycling race also shortened Sunday's stage by 30km (19 miles) as temperatures neared 40C.

Climate change is driving up temperatures around the world, and Europe is the fastest warming continent, heating up twice as fast as the global average, according to the Copernicus climate service.

This is causing increased summer heatwaves, greater pressure on Europe's water supply, and more intense wildfires.

The record-breaking temperatures across Europe this summer have led to major wildfires, most notably in Spain where at least 13 people were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Almeria, one of the country's deadliest ever.

And in the UK, a large wildfire in north Wales was declared a major incident by emergency services on Sunday, as firefighters tackled fires across England and Wales.

chineseTitle: 部长表示，巴黎南部肆虐的野火可能是故意纵火的
chineseSummary: 内政部长表示，巴黎附近的火灾可能是故意纵火，两人已被捕。
chineseContent:
消防员在巴黎附近的森林中扑灭野火

法国消防队员控制首都南部森林大火的战斗已进入第二天。

官方称枫丹白露森林大火“严重”且“规模异常”，导致该国主要的南北高速公路部分封闭。

内政部长劳伦特·努涅斯(Laurent Nunez)表示，大火可能是有人故意纵火的，此前官员称，大火已蔓延到巴黎东南部约40英里（60公里）的森林，面积达800公顷。

周一，努涅斯表示，已有两人因与枫丹白露火灾有关而被捕，全国各地有 59 人因与火灾有关而被捕。

巴黎地区正在经历今年夏季的第三次热浪，欧洲多个国家的气温记录都被打破。

法国全国消防员联合会的埃里克·布罗卡尔迪表示，这是消防飞机首次从通常较为干燥和炎热的南部地区派出前往巴黎地区扑灭火灾。

据法国通讯社法新社报道，他补充说，还部署了两架消防直升机和一架观察机。

“目的是拯救生命和财产，”他说。

观看：巴黎附近骑马中心大火逼近，马匹被疏散

努涅斯周一表示，当局正在探讨火灾是故意引发的可能性。

他在法新社报道的一份声明中说：“1000米范围内大约有10个起火点，这表明可能是有人故意设置的。”

努涅斯告诉法国第二台晚间八点新闻，两人因在该地区“故意或意外纵火”而被捕。

与此同时，法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙写道，枫丹白露森林“遭受了一场异常严重的火灾”。

他在社交媒体上写道：“所有可用资源都已被调动”，他表达了与当地居民的“声援”，并赞扬了扑灭大火的消防员。

早些时候，一场火灾还堵塞了一条从巴黎向东延伸的高速公路，并扰乱了一条通往法国南部的高铁线路。

法国铁路公司 SNCF 周日晚间表示，进出巴黎里昂火车站的旅客将面临长达六个小时的延误。

法国民事安全局局长朱利安·马里昂周五表示，今年以来，野火已覆盖法国约2.5万公顷土地。

法国最近的热浪迫使三座核电站暂时关闭，以避免温热的冷却水排放到过热的水道中。

由于气温接近 40 摄氏度，环法自行车赛的组织者也将周日的赛段缩短了 30 公里（19 英里）。

根据哥白尼气候服务机构的数据，气候变化正在推高世界各地的气温，而欧洲是变暖最快的大陆，升温速度是全球平均水平的两倍。

这导致夏季热浪加剧、欧洲供水压力更大以及野火更加猛烈。

今年夏天欧洲各地破纪录的气温导致了重大山火，尤其是在西班牙，周四阿尔梅里亚发生的山火造成至少 13 人死亡，这是该国有史以来最严重的山火之一。

在英国，周日，紧急服务部门宣布北威尔士发生的一场大火为重大事件，消防队员正在英格兰和威尔士各地扑灭大火。
